claim: N348I mutations cause resistance to zidovudine (AZT).

result.claim: N348I mutations cause resistance to zidovudine (AZT).
result.reasoning: The claim is supported by evidence from paper ID 15319019, titled "N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance." Proposition 3 from this paper explicitly states, "N348I decreased zidovudine susceptibility 2- to 4-fold in the context of wildtype HIV-1." The abstract further elaborates that N348I was highly associated with thymidine analogue mutations (TAMs) M41L and T215Y/F, and its selection was consistent with patients treated with zidovudine. The study involved both retrospective statistical analysis of clinical isolates and experimental introduction of the N348I mutation into HIV-1 molecular clones, providing a robust basis for the finding. While the paper's year and authors are unknown and it has 0 citations, the detailed methodology described in the abstract lends credibility to its findings.

evidence.text: PPM1D PTV mutations were present in 25 out of 7,781 cases versus 1 out of 5,861 controls.
evidence.source: Mosaic PPM1D mutations are associated with predisposition to breast and ovarian cancer

evidence.text: N348I was associated with non-nucleoside RTI resistance mutations K103N and Y181C/I.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: A retrospective statistical analysis of the Centre’s database identified an N348I mutation in the RT connection domain in treatment-experienced individuals.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: N348I decreased susceptibility to efavirenz 2.5-fold.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Somatic mutations activate phosphoinositide 3-kinase (PI3K) in the p110-alpha catalytic subunit (encoded by PIK3CA).
evidence.source: Effective Use of PI3K and MEK Inhibitors to Treat Mutant K-Ras G12D and PIK3CA H1047R Murine Lung Cancers

evidence.text: N348I increased in prevalence from below 1% in 368 treatment-naïve individuals to 12.1% in 1,009 treatment-experienced patients.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: N348I potentiated resistance to nevirapine and efavirenz when combined with K103N.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: N348I increased in prevalence from below 1% in 368 treatment-naive individuals to 12.1% in 1,009 treatment-experienced patients.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: N348I was associated with lamivudine resistance mutations M184V/I.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: The association with TAMs and NNRTI resistance mutations was consistent with the selection of N348I in patients treated with regimens that included both zidovudine and nevirapine.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: N348I was highly associated with thymidine analogue mutations M41L and T215Y/F.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: The mutations result in enhanced suppression of p53 in response to ionizing radiation exposure.
evidence.source: Mosaic PPM1D mutations are associated with predisposition to breast and ovarian cancer

evidence.text: NT5C2 mutant proteins conferred resistance to chemotherapy with 6-mercaptopurine when expressed in ALL lymphoblasts.
evidence.source: Activating mutations in the NT5C2 nucleotidase gene drive chemotherapy resistance in relapsed ALL

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 inhibited the proliferation of HER2-amplified BT474 cells expressing mutations E545K and H1047R.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: NT5C2 mutant proteins conferred resistance to chemotherapy with 6-thioguanine when expressed in ALL lymphoblasts.
evidence.source: Activating mutations in the NT5C2 nucleotidase gene drive chemotherapy resistance in relapsed ALL

evidence.text: N348I decreased zidovudine susceptibility 2- to 4-fold in the context of wildtype HIV-1.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: N348I decreased susceptibility to nevirapine 7.4-fold.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Mutant Ras was a determinant of resistance to PX-866.
evidence.source: Mutations in the phosphatidylinositol-3-kinase pathway predict for antitumor activity of the inhibitor PX-866 whereas oncogenic Ras is a dominant predictor for resistance.

evidence.text: Almost all known RT inhibitor resistance mutations identified to date map to the polymerase domain of the enzyme.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: The DNMTi 5-azacytidine (AZA) requires type I IFN response for effective in vivo antitumorigenic actions.
evidence.source: Epigenetic therapy activates type I interferon signaling in murine ovarian cancer to reduce immunosuppression and tumor burden.
